modules.name: mdit_py_plugins.amsmath
documentation: An extension to capture amsmath latex environments.

functions.name: mdit_py_plugins.amsmath.amsmath_plugin
documentation:
Parses TeX math equations, without any surrounding delimiters,
only for top-level [amsmath](https://ctan.org/pkg/amsmath) environments:

```latex
\begin{gather*}
a_1=b_1+c_1\\
a_2=b_2+c_2-d_2+e_2
\end{gather*}
```

```
param renderer

Function to render content, by default escapes HTML
```

modules.name: mdit_py_plugins.front_matter.index
documentation: Process front matter.

functions.name: mdit_py_plugins.front_matter.index.front_matter_plugin
documentation:
Plugin ported from
[markdown-it-front-matter](https://github.com/ParkSB/markdown-it-front-matter).

It parses initial metadata, stored between opening/closing dashes:

```md
---
valid-front-matter: true
---
```

functions.name: mdit_py_plugins.wordcount.basic_count
documentation: Split the string and ignore punctuation only elements.

functions.name: mdit_py_plugins.wordcount.wordcount_plugin
documentation:
Plugin for computing and storing the word count.

Stores in the `env` e.g.:

```
env["wordcount"] = {
  "words": 200
  "minutes": 1,
}
```

If "wordcount" is already in the env, it will update it.

```
param per_minute

Words per minute reading speed

param store_text

store all text under a "text" key, as a list of strings
```

functions.name: mdit_py_plugins.anchors.index.anchors_plugin
documentation:
Plugin for adding header anchors, based on [markdown-it-anchor](https://github.com/valeriangalliat/markdown-it-anchor) ```md # Title String ``` renders as: ```html

Title String ¶

``` ``` param min_level minimum header level to apply anchors param max_level maximum header level to apply anchors param slug_func function to convert title text to id slug. param permalink Add a permalink next to the title param permalinkSymbol the symbol to show param permalinkBefore Add the permalink before the title, otherwise after param permalinkSpace Add a space between the permalink and the title ``` Note, the default slug function aims to mimic the GitHub Markdown format, see: - -

functions.name: mdit_py_plugins.substitution.substitution_plugin
documentation:
A plugin to create substitution tokens.

These, token should be handled by the renderer.

Example:

```
{{ block }}

a {{ inline }} b
```

modules.name: mdit_py_plugins.container.index
documentation: Process block-level custom containers.

functions.name: mdit_py_plugins.container.index.container_plugin
documentation:
Plugin ported from
[markdown-it-container](https://github.com/markdown-it/markdown-it-container).

It is a plugin for creating block-level custom containers:

```md
:::: name
::: name
*markdown*
:::
::::
```

```
param name

the name of the container to parse

param marker

the marker character to use

param validate

func(marker, param) -> bool, default matches against the name

param render

render func
```

modules.name: mdit_py_plugins.tasklists
documentation: Builds task/todo lists out of markdown lists with items starting with [ ] or [x]

functions.name: mdit_py_plugins.tasklists.tasklists_plugin
documentation:
Plugin for building task/todo lists out of markdown lists with items starting with \[ \] or \[x\]
.. Nothing else

For example::
: - \[ \] An item that needs doing
  - \[x\] An item that is complete

The rendered HTML checkboxes are disabled; to change this, pass a truthy value into the enabled
property of the plugin options.

```
param enabled

True enables the rendered checkboxes

param label

True wraps the rendered list items in a <label> element for UX purposes,

param label_after

True adds the <label> element after the checkbox.
```

functions.name: mdit_py_plugins.myst_blocks.index.myst_block_plugin
documentation: Parse MyST targets (`(name)=`), blockquotes (`% comment`) and block breaks (`+++`).

functions.name: mdit_py_plugins.colon_fence.colon_fence_plugin
documentation:
This plugin directly mimics regular fences, but with `:` colons.

Example:

```
:::name
contained text
:::
```

functions.name: mdit_py_plugins.colon_fence._skipCharsStr
documentation: Skip character string from given position.

modules.name: mdit_py_plugins.admon.index
documentation: Process admonitions and pass to cb.

documentation: Separate the tag name from the admonition title.

functions.name: mdit_py_plugins.admon.index._validate
documentation: Validate the presence of the tag name after the marker.

functions.name: mdit_py_plugins.admon.index._extra_classes
documentation: Return the list of additional classes based on the markup.

functions.name: mdit_py_plugins.admon.index.admon_plugin
documentation:
Plugin to use
[python-markdown style admonitions](https://python-markdown.github.io/extensions/admonition).

```md
!!! note
    *content*
```

[And mkdocs-style collapsible blocks](https://squidfunk.github.io/mkdocs-material/reference/admonitions/#collapsible-blocks).

```md
???+ note
    *content*
```

Note, this is ported from
[markdown-it-admon](https://github.com/commenthol/markdown-it-admon).

variables.name: mdit_py_plugins.admon.index.MARKER_LEN
documentation: Regardless of extra characters, block indent stays the same

modules.name: mdit_py_plugins.field_list
documentation: Field list plugin

functions.name: mdit_py_plugins.field_list.fieldlist_plugin
documentation:
Field lists are mappings from field names to field bodies, based on the
[reStructureText syntax](https://docutils.sourceforge.io/docs/ref/rst/restructuredtext.html#field-lists).

```md
:name *markup*:
:name1: body content
:name2: paragraph 1

        paragraph 2
:name3:
  paragraph 1

  paragraph 2
```

A field name may consist of any characters except colons (":").
Inline markup is parsed in field names.

The field name is followed by whitespace and the field body.
The field body may be empty or contain multiple body elements.

Since the field marker may be quite long,
the second and subsequent lines of the field body do not have to
line up with the first line, but they must be indented relative to the
field name marker, and they must line up with each other.

functions.name: mdit_py_plugins.field_list.parseNameMarker
documentation:
Parse field name: `:name:`

```
returns

position after name marker, name text
```

documentation: Temporarily set parent type to `name`

functions.name: mdit_py_plugins.field_list.temp_state_changes
documentation: Allow temporarily changing certain state attributes.

modules.name: mdit_py_plugins.attrs.parse
documentation:
Parser for attributes:

```
attributes { id = "foo", class = "bar baz",
            key1 = "val1", key2 = "val2" }
```

Adapted from:
<https://github.com/jgm/djot/blob/fae7364b86bfce69bc6d5b5eede1f5196d845fd6/djot/attributes.lua#L1>

syntax:

attributes \<- '{' whitespace\* attribute (whitespace attribute)\* whitespace\* '}'
attribute \<- identifier | class | keyval
identifier \<- '#' name
class \<- '.' name
name \<- (nonspace, nonpunctuation other than ':', '\_', '-')+
keyval \<- key '=' val
key \<- (ASCII_ALPHANUM | ':' | '\_' | '-')+
val \<- bareval | quotedval
bareval \<- (ASCII_ALPHANUM | ':' | '\_' | '-')+
quotedval \<- '"' (\[^"\] | '"') '"'

functions.name: mdit_py_plugins.attrs.parse.parse
documentation:
Parse attributes from start of string.

:returns: (length of parsed string, dict of attributes)

methods.name: mdit_py_plugins.attrs.parse.TokenState.compile
documentation: compile the tokens into a dictionary

modules.name: mdit_py_plugins.deflist.index
documentation: Process definition lists.

functions.name: mdit_py_plugins.deflist.index.deflist_plugin
documentation:
Plugin ported from
[markdown-it-deflist](https://github.com/markdown-it/markdown-it-deflist).

The syntax is based on
[pandoc definition lists](http://johnmacfarlane.net/pandoc/README.html#definition-lists):

```md
Term 1
: Definition 1 long form

  second paragraph

Term 2 with *inline markup*
~ Definition 2a compact style
~ Definition 2b
```

functions.name: mdit_py_plugins.texmath.index.texmath_plugin
documentation:
Plugin ported from
[markdown-it-texmath](https://github.com/goessner/markdown-it-texmath).

It parses TeX math equations set inside opening and closing delimiters:

```md
$\alpha = \frac{1}{2}$
```

```
param delimiters

one of: brackets, dollars, gitlab, julia, kramdown
```

functions.name: mdit_py_plugins.dollarmath.index.dollarmath_plugin
documentation:
Plugin for parsing dollar enclosed math,
e.g. inline: `$a=1$`, block: `$$b=2$$`

This is an improved version of `texmath`; it is more performant,
and handles `\` escaping properly and allows for more configuration.

```
param allow_labels

Capture math blocks with label suffix, e.g. $$a=1$$ (eq1)

param allow_space

Parse inline math when there is space
after/before the opening/closing $, e.g. $ a $

param allow_digits

Parse inline math when there is a digit
before/after the opening/closing $, e.g. 1$ or $2.
This is useful when also using currency.

param allow_blank_lines

Allow blank lines inside $$. Note that blank lines are
not allowed in LaTeX, executablebooks/markdown-it-dollarmath, or the Github or
StackExchange markdown dialects. Hoever, they have special semantics if used
within Sphinx  admonitions, so are allowed for backwards-compatibility.

param double_inline

Search for double-dollar math within inline contexts

param label_normalizer

Function to normalize the label,
by default replaces whitespace with 

param renderer

Function to render content: ,
by default escapes HTML

param label_renderer

Function to render labels, by default creates anchor
```

functions.name: mdit_py_plugins.dollarmath.index.is_escaped
documentation: Test if dollar is escaped.

functions.name: mdit_py_plugins.dollarmath.index.math_inline_dollar
documentation:
Generate inline dollar rule.

```
param allow_space

Parse inline math when there is space
after/before the opening/closing $, e.g. $ a $

param allow_digits

Parse inline math when there is a digit
before/after the opening/closing $, e.g. 1$ or $2.
This is useful when also using currency.

param allow_double

Search for double-dollar math within inline contexts
```

functions.name: mdit_py_plugins.dollarmath.index.math_block_dollar
documentation: Generate block dollar rule.

modules.name: mdit_py_plugins.footnote.index
documentation: Process footnotes

functions.name: mdit_py_plugins.footnote.index.footnote_plugin
documentation:
Plugin ported from
[markdown-it-footnote](https://github.com/markdown-it/markdown-it-footnote).

It is based on the
[pandoc definition](http://johnmacfarlane.net/pandoc/README.html#footnotes):

```md
Normal footnote:

Here is a footnote reference,[^1] and another.[^longnote]

[^1]: Here is the footnote.

[^longnote]: Here's one with multiple blocks.

    Subsequent paragraphs are indented to show that they
belong to the previous footnote.
```

functions.name: mdit_py_plugins.footnote.index.footnote_def
documentation: Process footnote block definition
## RULES ##

functions.name: mdit_py_plugins.footnote.index.footnote_inline
documentation: Process inline footnotes (^[...])

functions.name: mdit_py_plugins.footnote.index.footnote_ref
documentation: Process footnote references ([^...])

functions.name: mdit_py_plugins.footnote.index.footnote_tail
documentation:
Post-processing step, to move footnote tokens to end of the token stream.

Also removes un-referenced tokens.

functions.name: mdit_py_plugins.footnote.index.render_footnote_anchor_name
documentation: Renderer partials

functions.name: mdit_py_plugins.attrs.index.attrs_plugin
documentation:
Parse inline attributes that immediately follow certain inline elements:

```
![alt](https://image.com){#id .a b=c}
```

This syntax is inspired by
[Djot spans](https://htmlpreview.github.io/?https://github.com/jgm/djot/blob/master/doc/syntax.html#inline-attributes).

Inside the curly braces, the following syntax is possible:

- `.foo` specifies foo as a class.
  Multiple classes may be given in this way; they will be combined.
- `#foo` specifies foo as an identifier.
  An element may have only one identifier;
  if multiple identifiers are given, the last one is used.
- `key="value"` or `key=value` specifies a key-value attribute.
  : Quotes are not needed when the value consists entirely of
    ASCII alphanumeric characters or `_` or `:` or `-`.
    Backslash escapes may be used inside quoted values.
- `%` begins a comment, which ends with the next `%` or the end of the attribute (`}`).

Multiple attribute blocks are merged.

```
param md

The MarkdownIt instance to modify.

param after

The names of inline elements after which attributes may be specified.
This plugin does not support attributes after emphasis, strikethrough or text elements,
which all require post-parse processing.

param spans

If True, also parse attributes after spans of text, encapsulated by .
Note Markdown link references take precedence over this syntax.

param span_after

The name of an inline rule after which spans may be specified.
```

functions.name: mdit_py_plugins.attrs.index.attrs_block_plugin
documentation:
Parse block attributes.

Block attributes are attributes on a single line, with no other content.
They attach the specified attributes to the block below them:

```
{.a #b c=1}
A paragraph, that will be assigned the class ``a`` and the identifier ``b``.
```

Attributes can be stacked, with classes accumulating and lower attributes overriding higher:

```
{#a .a c=1}
{#b .b c=2}
A paragraph, that will be assigned the class ``a b c``, and the identifier ``b``.
```

This syntax is inspired by Djot block attributes.

functions.name: mdit_py_plugins.attrs.index._find_opening
documentation: Find the opening token index, if the token is closing.

functions.name: mdit_py_plugins.attrs.index._attr_block_rule
documentation:
Find a block of attributes.

The block must be a single line that begins with a `{`, after three or less spaces,
and end with a `}` followed by any number if spaces.

functions.name: mdit_py_plugins.attrs.index._attr_resolve_block_rule
documentation: Find attribute block then move its attributes to the next block.
